title: Log Retention Index Frozen Bucket Age Compliance

grandmaExplanation: We report oldest searchable bucket age for data storing audit evidence so at least twelve months of immediately available history exists before cold or frozen archives ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `rest` search against Splunk cluster manager or monitoring console.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `| rest /services/data/indexes` ‚Äî `title`, `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `totalEventCount`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Adjust `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` to ‚â•395 days per policy (allow leap buffer). (2) Document frozen/archive retrieval for older periods. (3) Alert if `disabled=1` on PCI indexes. (4) Quarterly compliance sign-off screenshot.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| where match(title,"^(pci|cde|payment)_")
| eval retain_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,2)
| where retain_days<395
| table title, retain_days, totalEventCount, disabled
| sort retain_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Log Retention Index Frozen Bucket Age Compliance (PCI DSS Req 10.5.1, 10.5.1.2)** ‚Äî Reports oldest searchable bucket age for indexes storing PCI audit evidence so at least twelve months of immediately available history exists before cold or frozen archives.

Documented **Data sources**: `| rest /services/data/indexes` ‚Äî `title`, `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `totalEventCount`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `rest` search against Splunk cluster manager or monitoring console. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(title,"^(pci|cde|payment)_")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **retain_days** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where retain_days<395` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Log Retention Index Frozen Bucket Age Compliance (PCI DSS Req 10.5.1, 10.5.1.2)**): table title, retain_days, totalEventCount, disabled
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (index, retain_days), gauge (min retain_days), single value (indexes under policy).
